name: Todoist
tagline: Tasks, projects, and reminders your agent can manage.
description: Todoist is a task manager with a mature developer platform. Agents can create and complete tasks, organize projects, and query filters through the unified v1 REST API or the official hosted MCP server.

methods.label: Personal API token
methods.note: Best for personal scripts and single-user agents.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.note: Required for multi-user apps. Newer apps get expiring access tokens with rotating refresh tokens.

auth.guide:
**Personal token (fastest):** grab your API token from [Settings → Integrations → Developer](https://app.todoist.com/app/settings/integrations) in the Todoist app, then send it as a bearer token:

```bash
curl https://api.todoist.com/api/v1/tasks \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TODOIST_API_TOKEN"
```

**OAuth 2.0 (multi-user apps):** register an app in the [App Management Console](https://developer.todoist.com/appconsole.html), send users to `https://app.todoist.com/oauth/authorize` with `client_id`, `scope`, and `state`, then exchange the returned `code` at `https://api.todoist.com/oauth/access_token`. Scopes include `task:add`, `data:read`, `data:read_write`, `data:delete`, `project:delete`, and `backups:read`. Access tokens for newer apps expire after one hour and come with rotating refresh tokens.

**MCP:** the official server at `https://ai.todoist.net/mcp` uses OAuth — compatible clients (Claude Code, Cursor) open the consent screen for you on first use.

Note: Todoist's unified **API v1** (`https://api.todoist.com/api/v1/`) replaces the older REST v2 and Sync v9 APIs — new integrations should target v1.
